blog_posts.title: Behind the Scenes: Shooting a 45-Second Brand Spot in Times Square
blog_posts.excerpt: A look at how we lit and moved quickly for a high-pressure 45-second brand commercial in the middle of Times Square.
blog_posts.content: For this 45-second brand commercial, we had just four hours of permitted time in Times Square. In this article we break down our pre-light, lens choices, and how we managed reflections in LED billboards while keeping skin tones natural. We also share our camera movement plan and how we coordinated with NYPD to keep the crew nimble and safe.

blog_posts.title: Behind the Scenes: LED Volume Cinematography for a Music Video
blog_posts.excerpt: How we used an LED volume to create five distinct worlds for a single-day music video shoot.
blog_posts.content: Shooting in an LED volume gave us complete control over environments and reflections. In this breakdown, we cover our Unreal Engine workflows, color pipeline from on-set monitors to final grade, and practical tips for minimizing moiré on wardrobe. We also compare this approach to traditional green screen in terms of cost and schedule for indie artists.

blog_posts.title: Natural Light Strategies for Documentary Shoots
blog_posts.excerpt: Practical natural light techniques for documentaries when time, budget, and crew are limited.
blog_posts.content: Most documentary shoots don’t allow for big lighting setups. This article walks through our process for scouting with the sun, using negative fill and bounce in cramped spaces, and making small LED units feel invisible to non-actors. We include frame grabs from three recent docs and diagrams you can adapt to your own shoots.

blog_posts.title: Directing Performances in a 12-Minute New York Narrative Short
blog_posts.excerpt: Lessons learned from working with first-time actors on our award-winning New York short film.
blog_posts.content: Casting first-time actors for a narrative short can bring freshness and authenticity, but it also requires a different directing approach. In this case study, we share our rehearsal process, how we blocked naturally in cramped apartments, and the communication tricks that kept performances grounded during a tight three-day shoot.

blog_posts.title: How Our Branded Content Project Calculator Works
blog_posts.excerpt: A walkthrough of the assumptions behind our branded content estimator and how to read your results.
blog_posts.content: Our project calculator is designed to give marketing teams a realistic sense of cost, timeline, and deliverables before a single call is booked. We explain how we model crew size, post-production time, and licensing, and how the estimator adapts to your budget range, delivery timeline, and number of deliverables.

blog_posts.title: Luminary Films Opens Los Angeles Production Office
blog_posts.excerpt: We’re expanding with a new production hub in Los Angeles focused on commercials and music videos.
blog_posts.content: To better serve our West Coast clients, we’ve opened a dedicated production office in Los Angeles. The new space includes two edit bays, a color suite, and flexible production offices. Our LA team will focus on commercials, music videos, and branded content while collaborating closely with our New York documentary unit.

blog_posts.title: Our New York Narrative Short Takes Home Two Festival Awards
blog_posts.excerpt: “Midnight Delivery” wins Best Narrative Short and Best Cinematography at the 2021 East River Film Festival.
blog_posts.content: We’re honored that “Midnight Delivery,” our New York-set narrative short, has been recognized with two awards. In this post we share a few behind-the-scenes stills, our festival submission strategy, and what’s next for the team that made the film.

blog_posts.title: Cinematography Tactics for Small Crews and Big Results
blog_posts.excerpt: How we design lighting and camera packages that stay nimble without sacrificing production value.
blog_posts.content: Whether we’re shooting branded content or a doc series, many of our shoots are built around small, efficient crews. We share specific kit lists, lighting schemes, and crew roles that let us move fast while still delivering rich, cinematic images.

blog_posts.title: Behind the Scenes: Capturing Street Chefs for Our NYC Doc
blog_posts.excerpt: Inside our handheld, available-light approach to filming late-night street food culture in New York.
blog_posts.content: For “Street Chefs,” we spent six nights chasing steam, neon, and real stories around New York. We cover why we chose lightweight full-frame cameras, how we handled consent and release forms on the street, and the tricks we used to keep audio clean in chaotic environments.

blog_posts.title: What to Expect in Your First 30 Days Working With Us
blog_posts.excerpt: A week-by-week breakdown of how we move from discovery call to locked treatment.
blog_posts.content: Clear expectations make better projects. We outline our discovery, concept, and pre-production milestones so new clients know exactly how and when decisions get made, who they’ll be working with, and when to expect deliverables like treatments, storyboards, and production schedules.

jobs.title: Production Coordinator
jobs.description: We’re looking for a Los Angeles–based Production Coordinator to support our commercial, music video, and branded content shoots. You’ll work closely with producers to keep projects on schedule and on budget from bidding through wrap.
responsibilities: Coordinate shoot logistics including call sheets, crew bookings, and location details
Communicate daily with clients, vendors, and internal departments
Track budgets, POs, and invoices with the line producer
Maintain production calendars and ensure key milestones are met
Support on-set operations as needed during production days
qualifications: 2–5 years of production coordination experience in film, TV, or commercials
Comfortable managing multiple projects and deadlines simultaneously
Working knowledge of call sheets, production reports, and insurance requirements
Based in Los Angeles with ability to work on set when required

jobs.title: Post-Production Supervisor
jobs.description: The Post-Production Supervisor oversees all post-production workflows, ensuring projects move smoothly from edit through delivery.
responsibilities: Oversee edit, sound mix, color, and finishing for multiple concurrent projects
Build and maintain post-production schedules and workflows
Collaborate with directors and producers to maintain creative intent
Coordinate with external vendors for VFX, sound, and localization
Maintain media archives and delivery specs
qualifications: 5+ years of post-production experience, including client-facing work
Expertise in Adobe Premiere Pro or Avid, and familiarity with DaVinci Resolve
Strong understanding of codecs, color pipelines, and delivery formats

jobs.title: Creative Producer
jobs.description: The Creative Producer partners with clients and directors to develop concepts for commercials, branded content, and documentaries.
responsibilities: Lead creative development from brief to treatment for assigned projects
Collaborate with directors, writers, and strategists on pitches
Present creative concepts and treatments to clients
Work with production to scope budgets and timelines
Oversee creative through production and post to final delivery
qualifications: 3+ years in a creative producer or agency producer role
Experience presenting to clients and managing feedback
Understanding of production workflows and constraints

jobs.title: Marketing Coordinator
jobs.description: We’re seeking a detail-oriented Marketing Coordinator to support campaigns, social media, and case study production.
responsibilities: Assist in planning and executing digital marketing campaigns
Manage content calendar for social media and email newsletters
Coordinate case study and blog content with the creative team
Track performance metrics and prepare regular reports
qualifications: 1–3 years of marketing experience, preferably in media or creative industries
Familiarity with basic analytics tools and email marketing platforms

jobs.title: Office Manager
jobs.description: The Office Manager keeps our Los Angeles studio running smoothly, supporting both production and post teams.
responsibilities: Manage day-to-day office operations and supplies
Support HR with onboarding freelancers and staff
Coordinate meeting schedules and studio bookings
Serve as first point of contact for visitors and deliveries
qualifications: 2+ years of office management or operations experience
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, production environment

services.name: Music Videos
services.short_description: Concept-to-delivery music video production for labels and independent artists.
services.long_description: From performance pieces to narrative-driven visuals, our music video team handles creative development, production, and post. We specialize in stylized lighting, choreography coverage, and efficient single- or multi-day shoots tailored to artists’ release schedules and budgets.

services.name: Commercials
services.short_description: High-impact broadcast and digital spots for agencies and brands.
services.long_description: We produce spots from six-second bumpers to full :60 broadcast commercials. Our producers and directors collaborate with agencies and in-house teams to translate strategy into cinematic, on-brief creative that performs across TV, social, and OOH placements.

services.name: Branded Content
services.short_description: Documentary-style storytelling that builds brand affinity.
services.long_description: Our branded content team blends documentary techniques with brand strategy to create founder stories, customer profiles, and social-first series. We focus on real people, real locations, and repeatable production models that scale across campaigns and markets.

services.name: Documentaries
services.short_description: Short- and long-form documentaries from development through delivery.
services.long_description: We partner with broadcasters, streamers, and brands to develop and produce character-driven documentaries. Our services include story development, field production, post, and finishing, with flexible models for co-productions and branded editorial.

services.name: Production Process & Estimator
services.short_description: Transparent process, from first call to final delivery, plus a self-serve project calculator.
services.long_description: Our production process is built around clarity and collaboration. On this page, we walk through each phase—discovery, development, prep, production, and post—and offer a project calculator that helps you estimate budget, timeline, and deliverables for your next branded content piece.

services.name: Post-Production
services.short_description: Editing, color, sound, and finishing for existing footage or full productions.
services.long_description: Our post team offers edit, motion graphics, color grading, and sound finishing. Whether we produced your shoot or you’re bringing us existing footage, we build efficient workflows for social campaigns, trailers, sizzles, and trailers.

testimonials.summary: They translated a complex fintech product into a clear, cinematic story.
testimonials.full_text: The team helped us distill a very complex product into a simple, human story that our customers immediately understood. From the first treatment to the final grade, they were proactive, organized, and always thinking about performance across channels. The launch spot quickly became the centerpiece of our marketing and outperformed our previous creative by a wide margin.

testimonials.summary: Our founder series finally captured the heartbeat of our company.
testimonials.full_text: We wanted something more honest than a typical corporate video, and they delivered exactly that. The crew built real trust with our team and customers, which shows in every frame of the founder series. The episodes are now a key part of our sales process and get shared organically in investor updates.

testimonials.summary: Fast, social-first production that nailed our Gen Z audience.
testimonials.full_text: On an aggressive timeline, they delivered a punchy launch spot and a full set of social cutdowns without sacrificing craft. The creative team understood our Gen Z audience and worked seamlessly with our in-house designers on motion and UI elements. We hit our launch date with assets that felt native across TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube.

testimonials.summary: They made a complex R&D lab feel inviting and understandable.
testimonials.full_text: Biotech can easily come across as cold or overly technical, but this team found the human stories behind our research. They were meticulous about accuracy while still crafting a narrative that non-scientists could follow. The finished piece is now used in recruiting, investor pitches, and conference keynotes.

testimonials.summary: The Times Square spot gave our global launch a cinematic edge.
testimonials.full_text: Shooting overnight in Times Square was a logistical puzzle, but the production ran like clockwork. They balanced bold visuals with subtle product storytelling, and the spot cut together beautifully for both broadcast and social. Our retail partners still reference the commercial as a defining moment for the brand.

testimonials.summary: They showcased our sustainability story without feeling preachy.
testimonials.full_text: We needed to highlight our sustainability practices while still keeping the film emotional and aspirational. The crew spent real time on the factory floor and with our team, which led to moments that felt genuine rather than staged. The brand film helped us launch into new retailers and became our most-watched piece of content to date.

testimonials.summary: They treated our community with care and told our story with heart.
testimonials.full_text: Representation matters deeply to our organizers and volunteers, and the filmmakers understood that from day one. They listened more than they talked and made space for our community to lead the storytelling. The documentary has been an incredible tool for fundraising and has helped us secure new partners across the city.

testimonials.summary: A nuanced, visually stunning look at a difficult subject.
testimonials.full_text: Rising seas are a complex, emotionally charged topic, and the team approached it with sensitivity and rigor. They worked closely with our scientists and local communities to ensure accuracy without losing the human stakes. The film has become a cornerstone of our advocacy campaigns and educational programs.

testimonials.summary: They found poetry in the everyday experience of our riders.
testimonials.full_text: We commissioned this piece to celebrate the unseen rhythms of our transit system, and the result exceeded expectations. The filmmakers captured details our team walks past every day and organized them into a mesmerizing portrait of the network. It has been widely shared internally and with partner agencies around the world.
testimonials.author_title: Director of Communications, Metro Transit Authority

testimonials.summary: They respected the hustle and culture behind our late-night food scene.
testimonials.full_text: Our biggest concern was that an outside crew might sensationalize or oversimplify our vendors stories. Instead, they showed up night after night, built trust, and created a film that our community is genuinely proud of. The short documentary helped us secure new sponsors and a streaming partnership.

testimonials.summary: They turned a loose vibe reference into a fully realized visual world.
testimonials.full_text: I came in with a playlist and a Pinterest board, and they came back with a treatment that felt like they were inside my head. The shoot was smooth, the crew was supportive, and the final video helped the single break through on streaming and social. Fans still mention specific shots from the video at shows.

testimonials.summary: They know how to shoot choreography in a way dancers actually love.
testimonials.full_text: Our biggest priority was honoring the choreographers work, and the director and DP were completely aligned on that goal. They built blocking around the dancers instead of the other way around, and the long takes really showcase the performance. The video became a calling card for both the artist and the dance team.
